pitch.title: Final Girl Therapy
pitch.logline: A cynical support group of horror movie survivors must band together when a new, genre-savvy serial killer crashes their secluded wellness retreat.
pitch.core_theme: Overcoming past trauma and breaking out of expected roles.
pitch.synopsis: Act I: Chloe, a survivor of a summer camp massacre, reluctantly joins a wellness retreat for other final survivors. They spend their days swapping trauma stories and mocking horror tropes. Act II: A masked killer arrives, using their specific tropes against them. The survivors realize they cannot rely on their old survival tactics because the killer knows them all. Chloe steps up to lead, forcing the group to act like the first victims to confuse the killer. Act III: In a chaotic finale, the survivors turn the retreat into a trope-defying trap, unmasking the killer as their seemingly meek therapist who wanted to create the ultimate sequel.

beats.description: Chloe wakes from a hyper-violent summer camp nightmare, showcasing her severe PTSD and inability to move past her trauma.

Chloe reluctantly arrives at the secluded Pine Haven wellness retreat, meeting the meek therapist Dr. Vance and a cynical group of fellow Final Girls who immediately begin swapping survival stories and mocking horror tropes.
The group refuses to engage in actual therapy, instead bonding over their shared hyper-vigilance and pointing out all the structural flaws of the retreat's security.
The retreat's lone groundskeeper is found dead in a stylized, classic slasher setup, therefore confirming a new killer has trapped them in the woods without communication.
The Final Girls instantly snap into their respective veteran survival modes, effortlessly barricading doors and fashioning makeshift weapons.
The masked killer attacks but perfectly predicts their veteran maneuvers, using their specific franchise tropes against them and nearly slaughtering the entire group.
Retreating into the woods, the group realizes their battle-tested tactics are now fatal liabilities because the killer has studied all their movies.
Chloe steps up as the definitive leader, proposing a radical survival plan: they must act like clueless, doomed first-act victims to completely disrupt the killer's narrative expectations.
The hardened survivors awkwardly force themselves to split up, drink inappropriately, and loudly investigate strange noises in the dark.
The killer becomes deeply confused and frustrated by their bizarre, illogical behavior, which successfully causes the killer to hesitate and miss a massive ambush.
Furious at the broken rules, the killer abandons classic slasher tropes and pivots to brutal, unpredictable modern horror tactics.
The killer violently breaches their safe room, seemingly killing the group's toughest survivor and cornering Chloe, forcing her to relive the exact summer camp trauma she has been running from.
Chloe refuses to be a victim again, therefore she rallies the remaining survivors to stop running and weaponize the retreat's layout against the killer.
The survivors lure the killer into the main lodge using perfectly executed anti-tropes, trapping the murderer in a heavily fortified, inescapable dead-end.
The group subdues the killer, unmasking them as Dr. Vance, who maniacally monologues about orchestrating the ultimate sequel to cure their trauma through extreme exposure therapy.
Chloe interrupts the villain's monologue with a genre-defying one-liner, neutralizes Vance permanently, and leads the surviving women out of the woods, finally healed and ready to live normal lives.

characters.name: Chloe

psychological_core.desire: To finally feel safe enough to sleep through the night without waking up in a cold sweat.
psychological_core.false_belief: I am permanently broken and can only exist in a state of perpetual warfare.
psychological_core.fear: Being completely helpless and watching people die because she was not prepared.
psychological_core.need: To let go of her hyper-vigilance and realize she is more than just a survivor.
psychological_core.wound: The summer camp massacre where she was the sole survivor, bearing the crushing guilt of her friends' deaths.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She masks her severe PTSD by appearing bored and cynical. She does not scream or cry; instead, she quietly maps out exits, counts knives in the kitchen, and always positions herself facing the door. Her trauma only surfaces when someone touches her unexpectedly.

voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Pivoting to logistical or tactical observations. If asked about her emotional state, she will critique the structural integrity of the room's windows.
voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Blunt, clinical, and devoid of emotional adjectives. She NEVER says 'I am scared' or offers false reassurance like 'Everything will be fine.'

characters.name: Dr. Vance

psychological_core.desire: To orchestrate the ultimate, trope-defying horror sequel in real life.
psychological_core.false_belief: Extreme, life-threatening exposure therapy is the only way to cure trauma, making him a visionary psychological savior.
psychological_core.fear: Being dismissed as ordinary, mediocre, or a forgettable background character in someone else's story.
psychological_core.need: To face his own profound insignificance and lack of empathy, though he is too far gone to achieve this.
psychological_core.wound: A lifetime of being ignored, bullied, and overshadowed, leading to a pathological obsession with horror movie power dynamics.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He plays the meek, overly empathetic therapist flawlessly 90 percent of the time. His psychotic nature only bleeds through in his intense, unblinking eye contact and overly enthusiastic note-taking when the survivors describe their most gruesome, bloody memories.

voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Weaponizing therapy-speak. When challenged or cornered, he softly turns the question back on the accuser with phrases like 'Why do you feel the need to deflect with hostility?'
voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Soft-spoken, measured, and heavily reliant on academic psychology buzzwords. He NEVER raises his voice, interrupts, or uses profanity until his final unmasking in the third act.

characters.name: Lexi

psychological_core.desire: To prove she is invincible and can never be victimized or overpowered again.
psychological_core.false_belief: Physical strength and relentless aggression are the only valid shields against trauma.
psychological_core.fear: Vulnerability, emotional intimacy, and being perceived as weak by her peers.
psychological_core.need: To accept that relying on others and showing fear does not equate to weakness.
psychological_core.wound: A brutal, prolonged home invasion where she had to fight her way out with her bare hands, losing her family in the process.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She masks her deep-seated fear with aggressive humor and physical intimidation. She casually sharpens objects or exercises relentlessly during group therapy to avoid sitting still with her thoughts.

voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Using abrasive, sarcastic dark humor or initiating a physical challenge to completely derail vulnerable emotional conversations.
voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Loud, confrontational, and highly sarcastic. She NEVER asks for help, apologizes, or admits she does not know what to do.
